retriever: sophia's own wiki-corpus prose, sentence-split (offline; the deployment grounding source)

preRunMDE.note: MDE ~0.21 >> +0.05 floor at n=106/30-supported; reported BEFORE running per protocol

incumbentHealth.note: guards against FEVER-style baseline collapse (there lexical cov was 0.003)
incumbentHealth.verdict: INCUMBENT-DEGENERATE (margin uninterpretable) — lexical collapses on hedged provenance evidence

verdict: NOT GO — margin UNINTERPRETABLE. TWO pre-registered guards fired: (1) INCUMBENT-DEGENERATE — lexical coverage 0.0 < 0.10 floor (the token-overlap screen collapses on sophia's hedged provenance evidence 'attributed to'/'compiled', never firing against blunt 'X wrote Y' claims — the same FEVER-style collapse the clause guards against), so the NLI ΔF1 +0.34 (CI [0.11,0.48]) is NLI beating a collapsed baseline, NOT a real win; (2) UNDERPOWERED — n=106/30-supported, MDE ~0.21 >> +0.05 (declared pre-run). Manipulation check PASSED (100% fact-bearing wiki prose). The sophia-domain qualifier is NOT closed. Prerequisites are TWO, not one: (a) a larger curated evidence-groundable attribution pack (labeled data is scarce in sophia's domain), AND (b) a healthy incumbent — the lexical token-overlap screen cannot engage realistic hedged evidence, so a fair test needs a stronger baseline (e.g. a semantic/lexical hybrid incumbent) or the comparison is unfalsifiable.
